questions: Q1: What is the optimal content architecture for a pitch-deck data backbone that serves both investor slides and an updated website?
Q2: How should the financial model be framed to survive investor scrutiny while preserving the optimistic thesis?
Q3: What is the right level of product capability description — specific enough to be credible, abstract enough to follow user guidance?
Q4: How should the competitive landscape be structured to acknowledge Oracle's acceleration without undermining the thesis?
Q5: What evidence-quality tiers exist, and which claims need hedging vs. which can be stated as facts?
Q6: How should the four new continuation-context documents (Positioning Refined, Positioning Transcript, Reality Check, Tagline) shape the narrative arc?

answers.answer: The research report should follow a narrative arc optimized for sequential pitch-deck conversion: (1) The Structural Gap (problem framing via three-way thesis from Positioning Refined), (2) Market Opportunity (TAM with external citations), (3) What Helix Accomplishes (capabilities at category level), (4) Traction & Momentum (production metrics), (5) Financial Model (Dovie projections with stress-testing), (6) Competitive Moat (concentric compression model), (7) Risk Register with Mitigations (from Reality Check), (8) Team & Investment, (9) Branding Language (from Tagline), (10) Future Vision. This arc maps to standard investor deck flow while providing modular content blocks for website sections. Each section should be self-contained enough to become a slide or website section.
evidence: ticket.md — 'information that I'm going to feed in as a prompt to a pitch deck'
ticket.md — deliverable list: target market, TAM, money potential, positioning, threats, future
product/product.md — Success Criteria lists specific output categories
Helix_Positioning_Refined.pdf (via scout) — provides the three-way gap thesis as the narrative spine

answers.answer: The Dovie financial model should be presented in three layers: (1) Base case as-is (0-999 customers, $18M ARR at month 36, profitable month 6), (2) Stress-test commentary addressing four vulnerabilities (flat costs, zero churn, zero current revenue, compressed pricing), and (3) Upside framing highlighting $1,500/mo entry price as deliberate land-and-expand against $5K-$20K/mo consultant replacement value. The two investment structures (Dovie small raise vs. One-Pager larger raise) should be presented as complementary tiers targeting different investor profiles. The unverifiable Next Technik $60M exit comp should be positioned as directional reference only. Core financial argument: capital efficiency — profitable at 42 customers with $50K/mo burn is genuinely exceptional for enterprise SaaS.
evidence: diagnosis/apl.json A3 — detailed stress-testing of Dovie assumptions
scout/reference-map.json — no billing/subscription data in production database
product/product.md — financial model stress points table with recommended framings
Helix_AI_Dovie_Offer.pdf (via scout) — full 36-month model extracted

answers.answer: Product capabilities should be described using six high-level categories that map to the ownership thesis without referencing specific software implementations: (1) Autonomous operational lifecycle, (2) Governed execution, (3) Production inspection, (4) Account continuity, (5) Bidirectional communication, (6) Enterprise security. Each category anchors to a Manifesto principle (autonomous lifecycle to Completion, governed execution to Safety, account continuity to Continuity) to reinforce philosophical coherence. The 81% autonomous execution rate and 47% production deployment rate provide quantitative substance without requiring implementation details.
evidence: User continuation context — 'you don't have to point out specific software implementations'
product/product.md — six capability categories already defined at highest level
scout/reference-map.json — production metrics: 214/264 AUTO mode, 124/264 deployed
Helix_Manifesto.pdf (via scout) — 9 principles providing anchoring framework

answers.answer: The competitive landscape should use a concentric compression model rather than a feature comparison matrix. The key insight from Reality Check — 'Capability is abundant, governance is scarce' — is the centerpiece. ERP platforms compress the in-platform intelligence layer from one side (NetSuite Next, SuiteAgents, AI Connector). AI model vendors compress the capability layer from the other side (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot). Neither side claims end-to-end operational ownership with governed execution and durable account memory. Oracle's acceleration (NetSuite Next mid-2026, SuiteAgents) should be acknowledged head-on because it validates the market thesis — Oracle making ERPs smarter increases the complexity of the custom layer around them, making the governance role more valuable.
evidence: Reality_Check___Risks.pdf (via scout) — 'capability abundant, governance scarce'
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md — Oracle competitive intelligence with April 2026 web search data
Reality_Check___Risks.pdf (via scout) — 'Oracle is erasing superficial whitespace' but not claiming ownership layer
product/product.md — competitive landscape section with three threat tiers

answers.answer: Evidence quality falls into four tiers: (A) Hard facts — production metrics from runtime inspection, team composition, codebase capabilities; state directly. (B) Well-sourced external — NetSuite customer counts, market growth rates, Oracle announcements; state with citations. (C) Internal projections — Dovie financial model, pricing assumptions, exit scenarios; present as projections with transparent assumptions. (D) Unverifiable — Next Technik $60M price, distribution partner terms, churn behavior; explicit hedging language required. The research report should signal which tier each claim belongs to so the founder knows what to state confidently vs. frame carefully in investor conversations.
evidence: diagnosis/apl.json — evidence arrays trace each claim to specific sources
scout/reference-map.json unknowns — 11 explicitly identified unknowns
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md — Next Technik price 'NOT publicly disclosed'

answers.answer: The four new documents fundamentally strengthen the narrative structure: (1) Positioning Refined provides the narrative spine — the compressed five-line thesis is the single most pitch-ready piece of language in the entire evidence base. (2) Positioning Transcript provides raw founder voice and the 'ERP as database' future vision that belongs in the closing section. (3) Reality Check provides the 'why now' urgency with milestone-based kill rules that demonstrate founder discipline — investors value this. The 'less time than it feels like' warning becomes a capital urgency argument. (4) Tagline provides context-organized branding language ready for direct lift into slides and website. These four documents shift the report from data compilation toward narrative coherence — the structural gap thesis anchors everything, the Reality Check creates urgency, and the Tagline provides the language to land it.
evidence: Helix_Positioning_Refined.pdf (via scout) — compressed thesis statement
Helix_Positioning_Transcript.pdf (via scout) — ERP-as-database future, accountability gap
Reality_Check___Risks.pdf (via scout) — milestone gates at 3/6/12/18/36mo, 'less time than it feels like'
Helix_Tagline.pdf (via scout) — 5 finalists with context-specific recommendations

statement: This is a research ticket requiring a comprehensive information package to serve as the data backbone for an investor pitch deck and updated website. The technical direction focuses on content architecture, analytical framing decisions, and evidence quality management rather than software architecture. The research report should follow a narrative-arc structure (Problem/Structural Gap -> Insight -> Evidence -> Opportunity -> Ask) optimized for sequential pitch-deck conversion, with the compressed thesis from Positioning Refined as the narrative spine. Product capabilities should be described at six high-level categories without software implementation details, anchored to Manifesto principles. The financial model should be presented in three layers (base case, stress-test, upside framing). The competitive landscape should use the concentric compression model with 'governance is scarce' as the centerpiece. The Reality Check's milestone-based timeline provides the 'why now' urgency. All claims should be tagged to evidence quality tiers (hard facts, well-sourced, internal projections, unverifiable). No code changes are needed in any repository.
